data.title: Eagle Point Credit announces $100M debt offering via issuance of notes due 2030
data.description:
• Eagle Point Credit (NYSE:ECC) has priced an underwritten public offering of $100 million principal amount of its 7.75% notes due 2030, resulting in net proceeds of approximately $96.5 million.
  • Underwriters' overall allotment is an additional $15M aggregate principal amount of 2030 notes.
  • The notes will be issued in denominations of $25 and integral multiples of $25 in excess thereof. They will bear interest at a rate of 7.75% per year, payable quarterly, with the first interest payment occurring on March 31, 2025.
  • The closing date is December 10.
  • The company intends to list the 2030 New York Stock Exchange notes under the symbol "ECCU."

data.title: Amazon sued for allegedly excluding D.C. zip codes from Prime delivery benefits
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has been sued by Washington, D.C.'s attorney general for secretly excluding around 48,000 customers in low-income neighborhoods from its high-speed delivery service Prime while continuing to charge them the full subscription price.

D.C. Attorney General Brian Schwalb alleged that Amazon (AMZN) excluded Prime members in two zip codes (20019, 20020) east of the Anacostia River - "historically underserved communities" - from the fast delivery service since 2022.

The e-commerce giant instead has been servicing those zip codes with third-party delivery services like UPS (UPS) and the U.S. Postal Service, "which it knows are often slower than Amazon's," the lawsuit read.

It added that Amazon (AMZN) claims to have made "this secret change" in delivery options based on concerns over driver safety in the zip codes. But the company allegedly failed to be transparent about its decision.

"While Amazon (AMZN) has every right to make operational changes, it cannot covertly decide that a dollar in one zip code is worth less than a dollar in another," said Schwalb. "We're suing to stop this deceptive conduct and make sure District residents get what they're paying for."

data.title: European markets edged higher; Shell, Equinor to create joint UK venture
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.1%

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.2%

Factory orders in Germany declined by 1.5% mom in October, compared to market forecasts of a 2.0% fall.

France (CAC:IND)+0.6%

Industrial production in France fell for the second consecutive period by 0.1% month-on-month, easing from a revised 0.8% drop in September.

Industrial production in Spain, meanwhile, increased 1.9% in October over the same month in the previous year.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) ticked up +0.3% on Thursday, with focus on French markets as lawmakers voted to oust Prime Minister Michel Barnier and his cabinet through a no-confidence motion on Wednesday. Travel and banking stocks led gains, while industrials fell.

On the corporate front, the U.K.’s Competition and Markets Authority approved a merger between telecoms firms Vodafone (VOD) and Three UK, a subsidiary of CK Hutchison Holdings (OTCPK:CKHUY). Vodafone shares opened 0.6% higher.

Oil major Shell (SHEL) and Norway’s Equinor (EQNR) meanwhile announced plans to combine their British offshore oil and gas assets to create a jointly owned energy company in Scotland.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area retail sales, UK new car sales awaited shortly.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 2 basis points to 4.20%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points at 2.07%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 4.24%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: OGE Energy appoints Walworth as CFO
data.description:
• OGE Energy (NYSE:OGE) has announced the appointment of Charles Walworth as CFO and Treasurer, effective December 4, 2024.
  • Most recently, Walworth served as treasurer.
  • Additionally, the board approved a first quarter dividend of $0.42125 per common share of stock, to be paid on January 31, 2025, to shareholders of record on January 6, 2025.

data.title: Shell, Equinor to combine assets to form UK's biggest independent oil and gas firm
data.description:
British oil giant Shell (NYSE:SHEL) and Norway's state-run energy firm Equinor (NYSE:EQNR) will combine their U.K. offshore oil and gas assets to form a new company that will be the U.K.'s largest independent oil and gas producer.

The 50-50 joint venture is aimed at sustaining domestic oil and gas production and security of energy supply in the U.K. at a time when the U.K. North Sea basin is maturing and production is naturally declining.

A range of exploration licenses will also be part of the deal, the companies said in a statement. The JV is expected to produce over 140,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day in 2025.

"Domestically produced oil and gas is expected to have a significant role to play in the future of the U.K.'s energy system," said Zoë Yujnovich, Shell (SHEL) Integrated Gas and Upstream director. "To achieve this in an already mature basin, we are combining forces with Equinor."

"This transaction strengthens Equinor's (EQNR) near-term cash flow," Philippe Mathieu, the company's executive vice president for Exploration and Production International, said.

The transaction, which has economic effect on January 1, is expected to be completed by the end of 2025.

data.title: Amer Sports prices upsize 40.8M stock offering at $23
data.description:
• Amer Sports (NYSE:AS) has priced its upsized public offering by the issuance of 40.8M ordinary shares (from 34M) at $23/share.
  • Underwriters' overall allotment is an additional 6.12M ordinary shares.
  • The company plans to use the net proceeds from its offering to repay a portion of our outstanding borrowings under our term loan facilities.
  • The closing date is December 6.
  • AS is down 1.37% premarket to $25.24 on Thursday.

data.title: LGI Homes reports November home closings of 531
data.description:
• LGI Homes (NASDAQ:LGIH) on Wednesday announced it has closed 531 homes in November 2024, including the bulk sale of 103 single-family rental homes, compared to 525 homes closed in October.
  • As of November 30, 2024, the company had 142 active selling communities.

data.title: Vodafone UK - Three UK merger gets an all-clear, with conditions from CMA
data.description:
The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has approved Vodafone’s merger with Three, if both companies sign binding commitments.

Vodafone UK, unit of Vodafone Group Plc (NASDAQ:VOD) and Three UK, a subsidiary of CK Hutchison Holdings Limited (OTCPK:CKHUY) are 2 major providers of mobile telecommunication services in the UK. Last year both businesses announced a joint venture agreement which would bring tens of millions of customers – representing 27 million subscriptions - under a new, single network operator.

The commitments include to invest billions to roll out a combined 5G network across the UK along with the shorter term customer protections which would require the merged company to cap certain mobile tariffs and offer preset contractual terms to mobile virtual network operators, for a period of 3 years.

"Both Ofcom and the CMA would oversee the implementation of these legally binding commitments, which would help enhance the UK’s 5G capability whilst preserving effective competition in the sector," commented Stuart McIntosh, chair of the independent inquiry group leading the investigation.
